user_query:
Run an observability health assessment for our production AI agent fleet and produce actionable remediation. Use the provided inputs under input/.

1) Read these files:
- input/deployment.json — current deployment snapshot (agents running, monitoring in place, industry, alerting, spend)
- input/incidents.jsonl — last-30-day incident log (one JSON object per line)
- input/costs.csv — per-agent monthly cost breakdown

2) Based on these inputs, create these outputs:
- output/assessment.json — a structured 0–100 health assessment with:
  - overall "score" (0–100) and a "rating" using: 80–100 Production-grade, 60–79 Operational, 40–59 Risky, 0–39 Blind
  - dimension scores and rationales: execution_visibility (0–20), cost_attribution (0–20), output_quality (0–15), failure_recovery (0–15), security_boundaries (0–15), fleet_coordination (0–15). Ensure these sum to the overall score.
  - "benchmarks" section citing 2026 benchmarks per dimension
  - "industry_adjustments" for our "Ecommerce SaaS" context that reflect priority on Cost Attribution and Fleet Coordination
  - 3–7 "top_gaps" and brief evidence from the input files

- output/remediation.md — a prioritized 90-day monitoring roadmap tailored to our situation, including:
  - Headline: "90-Day Monitoring Roadmap"
  - Week 1–2, Week 3–4, Month 2, and Month 3 sections with concrete tasks
  - Targets for failure detection <5 min, cost anomaly flags, and scope violation alerts
  - Which of the 7 common monitoring mistakes apply and how we’ll avoid them

- output/cost_savings.json — estimate waste and net savings using the 2026 cost framework for our fleet size, with fields:
  {
    "company_size_bracket": "20-100 agents",
    "unmonitored_waste_usd": {"min": <number>, "max": <number>},
    "monitoring_investment_usd": {"min": <number>, "max": <number>},
    "net_savings_usd": {"min": <number>, "max": <number>},
    "current_spend_usd": <number>,
    "estimate_method": "framework_2026"
  }
  Ensure the bracket matches 20–100 agents; keep values within the published ranges for that bracket and make net_savings consistent with the other ranges.

- output/compliance_mapping.json — a lightweight mapping of our planned monitoring controls to at least OWASP Agentic Top 10 and NIST AI RMF, with fields:
  {
    "frameworks": ["OWASP Agentic Top 10", "NIST AI RMF"],
    "mappings": {
      "OWASP Agentic Top 10": {"coverage": <0–100>, "status": "<string>", "controls": [{"id": "<control>", "status": "<string>"}]},
      "NIST AI RMF": {"coverage": <0–100>, "status": "<string>", "controls": [{"id": "<function|category>", "status": "<string>"}]}
    },
    "notes": "<short rationale>"
  }

Please make the assessment defensible with references to the input data, ensure the dimension scores are consistent with the overall score and rating, and tailor the roadmap and priorities to an Ecommerce + SaaS fleet of ~27 agents.
